metadata.note: Ideal nonrelativistic n=2 Stark model; parity and delta-m filters separated; real-hydrogen and line-intensity caveats explicit.

beats.label: Parity eliminates same-parity entries because z is odd. Azimuthal symmetry also enforces delta m equ

beats.label: Diagonalizing the surviving two-by-two block gives equal superpositions of two s and two p zero. The

beats.prompt: At ten to the fifth volts per centimeter, three a-nought e script-E is about sixteen millielectron-volts. That is the ideal first-order scale, before asking whe

beats.prompt: Your turn. If the field is cut in half, what happens to the plus-or-minus first-order Stark shift in this linear regime?
